enonce.texte:
Read the sentences below and choose the correct fundraising vocabulary word to complete each sentence.

1. Our school needs to _______ money for new classroom equipment.

2. The _______ will help build a new library for the community.

3. We need everyone's _______ to make this event successful.

4. Please _______ to the cause by donating whatever you can.

5. The _______ raised will be used to support local charities.

questions.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct fundraising vocabulary.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 1 with the verb meaning to collect money.
sousQuestions.reponse: Our school needs to raise money for new classroom equipment.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 2 with the word for a planned undertaking.
sousQuestions.reponse: The project will help build a new library for the community.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 3 with the word for assistance or backing.
sousQuestions.reponse: We need everyone's support to make this event successful.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 4 with the verb meaning to give or donate.
sousQuestions.reponse: Please contribute to the cause by donating whatever you can.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete sentence 5 with the word for money raised for a purpose.
sousQuestions.reponse: The funds raised will be used to support local charities.

enonce.texte:
Read the text about a fundraising speech and answer the questions.

Preparing a Fundraising Speech

A fundraising speech is a persuasive talk delivered to an audience to encourage them to donate money to a cause. The speaker must clearly explain the purpose of the project, why it is important, and how the funds will be used. A successful speech uses emotional language to connect with the audience and inspires them to take action. Future tenses like 'will' and 'going to' are often used to express intentions and promises. The speaker should also thank the audience for their support and invite them to contribute.

questions.texte: Answer the following questions based on the text.
questions.reponse:
1. To encourage them to donate money to a cause.
2. Explain the purpose, importance, and use of funds.
3. It uses emotional language and inspires action.
4. Express intentions and promises.
5. Thank the audience and invite them to contribute.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the purpose of a fundraising speech?
sousQuestions.reponse: To encourage the audience to donate money to a cause.

sousQuestions.texte: What should the speaker explain in the speech?
sousQuestions.reponse: The purpose of the project, why it is important, and how the funds will be used.

sousQuestions.texte: What makes a fundraising speech successful?
sousQuestions.reponse: Using emotional language and inspiring the audience to take action.

sousQuestions.texte: Why are future tenses used in fundraising speeches?
sousQuestions.reponse: To express intentions and promises about the project.

sousQuestions.texte: What should the speaker do at the end of the speech?
sousQuestions.reponse: Thank the audience and invite them to contribute.

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences with the correct future tense: will or going to.

questions.texte: Choose the correct future tense for each sentence.
questions.reponse:
1. are going to
2. will
3. is going to
4. will
5. are going to

sousQuestions.texte: We _______ (raise) funds for the school project next month.
sousQuestions.reponse: are going to raise

sousQuestions.texte: I _______ (help) you with the event planning.
sousQuestions.reponse: will help

sousQuestions.texte: She _______ (present) the speech at the fundraising dinner.
sousQuestions.reponse: is going to present

sousQuestions.texte: The community _______ (benefit) from these efforts.
sousQuestions.reponse: will benefit

sousQuestions.texte: They _______ (donate) the money to a local charity.
sousQuestions.reponse: are going to donate

enonce.texte:
Read the following excerpt from a fundraising speech and answer the questions.

Speech Excerpt:

Good evening, ladies and gentlemen. I am here tonight to talk about a project that is very close to my heart. We have a dream to build a community library for the children of our neighborhood. This library will be a place where young minds can explore, learn, and grow. We are going to raise funds to buy books, computers, and furniture. Your support will make this dream a reality. I am asking each of you to contribute. Together, we can change lives. Thank you for your generosity.

questions.texte: Analyze the speech excerpt and answer the questions.
questions.reponse:
1. The speaker greets the audience.
2. To build a community library for children.
3. Books, computers, and furniture.
4. 'We are going to raise funds' and 'Your support will make this dream a reality.'
5. It inspires the audience and encourages them to contribute.

sousQuestions.texte: How does the speaker begin the speech?
sousQuestions.reponse: The speaker greets the audience by saying 'Good evening, ladies and gentlemen.'

sousQuestions.texte: What is the purpose of the project?
sousQuestions.reponse: To build a community library for children.

sousQuestions.texte: What will the funds be used for?
sousQuestions.reponse: To buy books, computers, and furniture.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify two examples of future tenses used in the speech.
sousQuestions.reponse: 'We are going to raise funds' and 'Your support will make this dream a reality.'

sousQuestions.texte: What is the effect of the phrase 'Together, we can change lives'?
sousQuestions.reponse: It inspires the audience and creates a sense of unity and purpose.

enonce.texte:
Write a fundraising speech to raise money for a school project (e.g., building a new classroom, buying sports equipment, or supporting a school club).

Instructions:

  • Greet the audience
  • Explain the purpose of the project and why it is important
  • Explain how the funds will be used
  • Use emotional and persuasive language
  • Include a call to action
  • Use at least 5 future tense sentences (will or going to)

questions.reponse: A well-structured speech with greeting, explanation of the project, use of funds, persuasive language, call to action, and at least 5 future tense sentences.

sousQuestions.texte: Greet the audience appropriately.
sousQuestions.reponse: Good morning/afternoon/evening, dear teachers, parents, and fellow students.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain the purpose of the project.
sousQuestions.reponse: We are here today to raise funds for a new computer lab that will help students learn digital skills.

sousQuestions.texte: Explain why the project is important.
sousQuestions.reponse: This computer lab will provide our students with the tools they need to succeed in the modern world.

sousQuestions.texte: Describe how the funds will be used.
sousQuestions.reponse: The money will be used to buy computers, furniture, and software for the lab.

sousQuestions.texte: Include a call to action.
sousQuestions.reponse: I urge you all to contribute generously. Together, we can make this dream a reality.

sousQuestions.texte: Use at least 5 future tense sentences.
sousQuestions.reponse: We are going to transform our school. We will buy computers. We will install software. Students will learn new skills. We will create a brighter future.
